verses.latin_vulgate: Pepigi fœdus cum oculis meis,  ut ne cogitarem quidem de virgine.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּ֭רִית כָּרַ֣תִּי לְעֵינָ֑י וּמָ֥ה אֶ֝תְבּוֹנֵ֗ן עַל־בְּתוּלָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: I made a covenant with my eyes, that I would not so much as think upon a virgin.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä olen tehnyt liiton silmäini kanssa, etten minä katsoisi neitseen päälle.
verses.king_james: I made a covenant with mine eyes; why then should I think upon a maid?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quam enim partem haberet in me Deus desuper,  et hæreditatem Omnipotens de excelsis ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּמֶ֤ה חֵ֣לֶק אֱל֣וֹהַּ מִמָּ֑עַל וְֽנַחֲלַ֥ת שַׁ֝דַּ֗י מִמְּרֹמִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: For what part should God from above have in me, and what inheritance the Almighty from on high?
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta mitä Jumala ylhäältä antaa minulle osaksi ja Kaikkivaltias korkialta perinnöksi?
verses.king_james: For what portion of God is there from above? and what inheritance of the Almighty from on high?

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid non perditio est iniquo,  et alienatio operantibus injustitiam ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲלֹא־אֵ֥יד לְעַוָּ֑ל וְ֝נֵ֗כֶר לְפֹ֣עֲלֵי אָֽוֶן׃
verses.douay_rheims: Is not destruction to the wicked, and aversion to them that work iniquity?
verses.finnish_biblia: Eikö väärän pitäisi näkemän senkaltaista vaivaisuutta, ja pahantekiän senkaltaista surkeutta kärsimän?
verses.king_james: Is not destruction to the wicked? and a strange punishment to the workers of iniquity?

verses.latin_vulgate: Nonne ipse considerat vias meas,  et cunctos gressus meos dinumerat ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲלֹא־ה֭וּא יִרְאֶ֣ה דְרָכָ֑י וְֽכׇל־צְעָדַ֥י יִסְפּֽוֹר׃
verses.douay_rheims: Doth not he consider my ways, and number all my steps?
verses.finnish_biblia: Eikö hän näe minun teitäni ja lueskele kaikkia minun askeleitani?
verses.king_james: Doth not he see my ways, and count all my steps?

verses.latin_vulgate: Si ambulavi in vanitate,  et festinavit in dolo pes meus,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־הָלַ֥כְתִּי עִם־שָׁ֑וְא וַתַּ֖חַשׁ עַל־מִרְמָ֣ה רַגְלִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: If I have walked in vanity, and my foot hath made haste to deceit:
verses.finnish_biblia: Olenko minä vaeltanut turhassa menossa, eli minun jalkani kiiruhtaneet petokseen?
verses.king_james: If I have walked with vanity, or if my foot hath hasted to deceit;

verses.latin_vulgate: appendat me in statera justa,  et sciat Deus simplicitatem meam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יִשְׁקְלֵ֥נִי בְמֹֽאזְנֵי־צֶ֑דֶק וְיֵדַ֥ע אֱ֝ל֗וֹהַּ תֻּמָּתִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Let him weigh me in a just balance, and let God know my simplicity.
verses.finnish_biblia: Punnitkaan hän minua oikialla vaa'alla, niin Jumala ymmärtää minun vakuuteni.
verses.king_james: Let me be weighed in an even balance, that God may know mine integrity.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si declinavit gressus meus de via,  et si secutum est oculos meos cor meum,  et si manibus meis adhæsit macula,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִ֥ם־תִּטֶּ֣ה אַשֻּׁרִי֮ מִנִּ֢י הַ֫דָּ֥רֶךְ וְאַחַ֣ר עֵ֭ינַי הָלַ֣ךְ לִבִּ֑י וּ֝בְכַפַּ֗י דָּ֣בַק מֽאוּם׃
verses.douay_rheims: If my step hath turned out of the way, and if my heart hath followed my eyes, and if a spot hath cleaved to my hands:
verses.finnish_biblia: Jos minun askeleeni ovat poikenneet tieltä, ja minun sydämeni seurannut silmiäni, ja jotakin riippunut minun käsissäni,
verses.king_james: If my step hath turned out of the way, and mine heart walked after mine eyes, and if any blot hath cleaved to mine hands;

verses.latin_vulgate: seram, et alius comedat,  et progenies mea eradicetur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶ֭זְרְעָה וְאַחֵ֣ר יֹאכֵ֑ל וְֽצֶאֱצָאַ֥י יְשֹׁרָֽשׁוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then let me sow and let another reap: and let my offspring be rooted out.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin minä kylväisin ja toinen söis, ja minun sikiäni hukkuis juurinensa.
verses.king_james: Then let me sow, and let another eat; yea, let my offspring be rooted out.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si deceptum est cor meum super muliere,  et si ad ostium amici mei insidiatus sum,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־נִפְתָּ֣ה לִ֭בִּי עַל־אִשָּׁ֑ה וְעַל־פֶּ֖תַח רֵעִ֣י אָרָֽבְתִּי׃
verses.douay_rheims: If my heart hath been deceived upon a woman, and if I have laid wait at my friend's door:
verses.finnish_biblia: Jos minun sydämeni on vietelty vaimon perään, ja olen väijynyt lähimmäiseni ovella,
verses.king_james: If mine heart have been deceived by a woman, or if I have laid wait at my neighbour’s door;

verses.latin_vulgate: scortum alterius sit uxor mea,  et super illam incurventur alii.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תִּטְחַ֣ן לְאַחֵ֣ר אִשְׁתִּ֑י וְ֝עָלֶ֗יהָ יִכְרְע֥וּן אֲחֵרִֽין׃
verses.douay_rheims: Let my wife be the harlot of another, and let other men lie with her.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin minun emäntäni häväistäkään muilta, ja muut maatkaan hänen;
verses.king_james: Then let my wife grind unto another, and let others bow down upon her.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hoc enim nefas est,  et iniquitas maxima.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּי־[הִ֥יא] (הוא) זִמָּ֑ה (והיא) [וְ֝ה֗וּא] עָוֺ֥ן פְּלִילִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: For this is a heinous crime, and a most grievous iniquity.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä se on häpiä ja paha työ tuomarien edessä;
verses.king_james: For this is an heinous crime; yea, it is an iniquity to be punished by the judges.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ignis est usque ad perditionem devorans,  et omnia eradicans genimina.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י אֵ֣שׁ הִ֭יא עַד־אֲבַדּ֣וֹן תֹּאכֵ֑ל וּֽבְכׇל־תְּב֖וּאָתִ֣י תְשָׁרֵֽשׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: It is a fire that devoureth even to destruction, and rooteth up all things that spring.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä se olis tuli joka polttais kadotukseen, ja kaiken minun saatuni peräti hukuttais.
verses.king_james: For it is a fire that consumeth to destruction, and would root out all mine increase.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si contempsi subire judicium cum servo meo et ancilla mea,  cum disceptarent adversum me :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־אֶמְאַ֗ס מִשְׁפַּ֣ט עַ֭בְדִּי וַאֲמָתִ֑י בְּ֝רִבָ֗ם עִמָּדִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: If I have despised to abide judgment with my manservant, or my maidservant, when they had any controversy against me:
verses.finnish_biblia: Jos olen katsonut ylön palveliani eli palkkapiikani oikeuden, riidellessänsä minun kanssani,
verses.king_james: If I did despise the cause of my manservant or of my maidservant, when they contended with me;

verses.latin_vulgate: quid enim faciam cum surrexerit ad judicandum Deus ?  et cum quæsierit, quid respondebo illi ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּמָ֣ה אֶ֭עֱשֶׂה כִּֽי־יָק֣וּם אֵ֑ל וְכִי־יִ֝פְקֹ֗ד מָ֣ה אֲשִׁיבֶֽנּוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: For what shall I do when God shall rise to judge? and when he shall examine, what shall I answer him?
verses.finnish_biblia: Mitä minä sitte tekisin kuin Jumala nousee? eli mitä minä vastaisin häntä, kuin hän kostaa?
verses.king_james: What then shall I do when God riseth up? and when he visiteth, what shall I answer him?

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid non in utero fecit me, qui et illum operatus est,  et formavit me in vulva unus ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲֽלֹא־בַ֭בֶּטֶן עֹשֵׂ֣נִי עָשָׂ֑הוּ וַ֝יְכֻנֶ֗נּוּ בָּרֶ֥חֶם אֶחָֽד׃
verses.douay_rheims: Did not he that made me in the womb make him also: and did not one and the same form me in the womb?
verses.finnish_biblia: Eikö hän ole tehnyt häntä, joka minunkin äitini kohdussa teki? ja on meidät molemmat kohdussa valmistanut.
verses.king_james: Did not he that made me in the womb make him? and did not one fashion us in the womb?

verses.latin_vulgate: Si negavi quod volebant pauperibus,  et oculos viduæ expectare feci ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־אֶ֭מְנַע מֵחֵ֣פֶץ דַּלִּ֑ים וְעֵינֵ֖י אַלְמָנָ֣ה אֲכַלֶּֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: If I have denied to the poor what they desired, and have made the eyes of the widow wait:
verses.finnish_biblia: Olenko minä kieltänyt tarvitsevaisilta, mitä he minulta ovat pyytäneet, ja antanut leskein silmät heikoksi tulla?
verses.king_james: If I have withheld the poor from their desire, or have caused the eyes of the widow to fail;

verses.latin_vulgate: si comedi buccellam meam solus,  et non comedit pupillus ex ea
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאֹכַ֣ל פִּתִּ֣י לְבַדִּ֑י וְלֹֽא־אָכַ֖ל יָת֣וֹם מִמֶּֽנָּה׃
verses.douay_rheims: If I have eaten my morsel alone, and the fatherless hath not eaten thereof:
verses.finnish_biblia: Olenko minä syönyt palani yksinäni, ettei orpo ole myös siitä syönyt?
verses.king_james: Or have eaten my morsel myself alone, and the fatherless hath not eaten thereof;

verses.latin_vulgate: (quia ab infantia mea crevit mecum miseratio,  et de utero matris meæ egressa est mecum) ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י מִ֭נְּעוּרַי גְּדֵלַ֣נִי כְאָ֑ב וּמִבֶּ֖טֶן אִמִּ֣י אַנְחֶֽנָּה׃
verses.douay_rheims: (For from my infancy mercy grew up with me: and it came out with me from my mother's womb:)
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä nuoruudestani olen minä ollut niinkuin isä, ja hamasta äitini kohdusta olen minä mielelläni holhonnut.
verses.king_james: (For from my youth he was brought up with me, as with a father, and I have guided her from my mother’s womb;)

verses.latin_vulgate: si despexi pereuntem, eo quod non habuerit indumentum,  et absque operimento pauperem ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־אֶרְאֶ֣ה א֭וֹבֵד מִבְּלִ֣י לְב֑וּשׁ וְאֵ֥ין כְּ֝ס֗וּת לָאֶבְיֽוֹן׃
verses.douay_rheims: If I have despised him that was perishing for want of clothing, and the poor man that had no covering:
verses.finnish_biblia: Jos minä olen nähnyt jonkun hukkuvan, ettei hänellä ollut vaatetta, ja sallinut käydä köyhän peittämättä;
verses.king_james: If I have seen any perish for want of clothing, or any poor without covering;

verses.latin_vulgate: si non benedixerunt mihi latera ejus,  et de velleribus ovium mearum calefactus est ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־לֹ֣א בֵרְכ֣וּנִי חֲלָצָ֑ו וּמִגֵּ֥ז כְּ֝בָשַׂ֗י יִתְחַמָּֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: If his sides have not blessed me, and if he were not warmed with the fleece of my sheep:
verses.finnish_biblia: Jos ei hänen lanteensa ole siunannut minua, kuin hän minun lammasnahoillani lämmitettiin;
verses.king_james: If his loins have not blessed me, and if he were not warmed with the fleece of my sheep;

verses.latin_vulgate: si levavi super pupillum manum meam,  etiam cum viderem me in porta superiorem :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־הֲנִיפ֣וֹתִי עַל־יָת֣וֹם יָדִ֑י כִּֽי־אֶרְאֶ֥ה בַ֝שַּׁ֗עַר עֶזְרָתִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: If I have lifted up my hand against the fatherless, even when I saw myself superior in the gate:
verses.finnish_biblia: Jos olen nostanut käteni orpoja vastaan, ehkä minä näin minun voimallisena porteissa olevan;
verses.king_james: If I have lifted up my hand against the fatherless, when I saw my help in the gate:

verses.latin_vulgate: humerus meus a junctura sua cadat,  et brachium meum cum suis ossibus confringatur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כְּ֭תֵפִי מִשִּׁכְמָ֣הֿ תִפּ֑וֹל וְ֝אֶזְרֹעִ֗י מִקָּנָ֥הֿ תִשָּׁבֵֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: Let my shoulder fall from its joint, and let my arm with its bones be broken.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin kaatukoon minun hartiani lapaluiltani, ja minun käsivarteni särkyköön luinensa.
verses.king_james: Then let mine arm fall from my shoulder blade, and mine arm be broken from the bone.

verses.latin_vulgate: Semper enim quasi tumentes super me fluctus timui Deum,  et pondus ejus ferre non potui.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י פַ֣חַד אֵ֭לַי אֵ֣יד אֵ֑ל וּ֝מִשְּׂאֵת֗וֹ לֹ֣א אוּכָֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: For I have always feared God as waves swelling over me, and his weight I was unable to bear.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä Jnmalan rangaistus on minulle vavistukseksi, ja en taida hänen korkeuttansa välttää.
verses.king_james: For destruction from God was a terror to me, and by reason of his highness I could not endure.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si putavi aurum robur meum,  et obrizo dixi : Fiducia mea ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־שַׂ֣מְתִּי זָהָ֣ב כִּסְלִ֑י וְ֝לַכֶּ֗תֶם אָמַ֥רְתִּי מִבְטַחִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: If I have thought gold my strength, and have said to fine gold: My confidence:
verses.finnish_biblia: Olenko minä asettanut kullan turvakseni? ja sanonut puhtaalle kullalle: sinä olet minun uskallukseni?
verses.king_james: If I have made gold my hope, or have said to the fine gold, Thou art my confidence;

verses.latin_vulgate: si lætatus sum super multis divitiis meis,  et quia plurima reperit manus mea ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־אֶ֭שְׂמַח כִּֽי־רַ֣ב חֵילִ֑י וְכִֽי־כַ֝בִּ֗יר מָצְאָ֥ה יָדִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: If I have rejoiced over my great riches, and because my hand had gotten much.
verses.finnish_biblia: Olenko minä iloinnut tavarani paljoudesta, ja että käteni paljon riistaa koonneet ovat?
verses.king_james: If I rejoiced because my wealth was great, and because mine hand had gotten much;

verses.latin_vulgate: si vidi solem cum fulgeret,  et lunam incedentem clare,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־אֶרְאֶ֣ה א֭וֹר כִּ֣י יָהֵ֑ל וְ֝יָרֵ֗חַ יָקָ֥ר הֹלֵֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: If I beheld the sun when it shined and the moon going in brightness:
verses.finnish_biblia: Olenko minä katsonut valkeutta, koska se kirkkaasti paisti, ja kuuta, koska se täydellinen oli?
verses.king_james: If I beheld the sun when it shined, or the moon walking in brightness;

verses.latin_vulgate: et lætatum est in abscondito cor meum,  et osculatus sum manum meam ore meo :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּ֣פְתְּ בַּסֵּ֣תֶר לִבִּ֑י וַתִּשַּׁ֖ק יָדִ֣י לְפִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And my heart in secret hath rejoiced, and I have kissed my hand with my mouth:
verses.finnish_biblia: Onko minun sydämeni salaa minua vietellyt, suuta antamaan kädelleni?
verses.king_james: And my heart hath been secretly enticed, or my mouth hath kissed my hand:

verses.latin_vulgate: quæ est iniquitas maxima,  et negatio contra Deum altissimum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּם־ה֭וּא עָוֺ֣ן פְּלִילִ֑י כִּֽי־כִחַ֖שְׁתִּי לָאֵ֣ל מִמָּֽעַל׃
verses.douay_rheims: Which is a very great iniquity, and a denial against the most high God.
verses.finnish_biblia: Joka myös vääryys on tuomarien edessä; sillä niin olisin minä kieltänyt Jumalan ylhäältä.
verses.king_james: This also were an iniquity to be punished by the judge: for I should have denied the God that is above.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si gavisus sum ad ruinam ejus qui me oderat,  et exsultavi quod invenisset eum malum :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־אֶ֭שְׂמַח בְּפִ֣יד מְשַׂנְאִ֑י וְ֝הִתְעֹרַ֗רְתִּי כִּֽי־מְצָ֥אוֹ רָֽע׃
verses.douay_rheims: If I have been glad at the downfall of him that hated me, and have rejoiced that evil had found him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Olenko minä iloinnut viholliseni vastoinkäymisestä? taikka riemuinnut, että onnettomuus tuli hänen päällensä?
verses.king_james: If I rejoiced at the destruction of him that hated me, or lifted up myself when evil found him:

verses.latin_vulgate: non enim dedi ad peccandum guttur meum,  ut expeterem maledicens animam ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלֹא־נָתַ֣תִּי לַחֲטֹ֣א חִכִּ֑י לִשְׁאֹ֖ל בְּאָלָ֣ה נַפְשֽׁוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: For I have not been given my mouth to sin, by wishing a curse to his soul.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä en minä antanut minun suuni syntiä tehdä, sadatellakseni hänen sieluansa.
verses.king_james: Neither have I suffered my mouth to sin by wishing a curse to his soul.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si non dixerunt viri tabernaculi mei :  Quis det de carnibus ejus, ut saturemur ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־לֹ֣א אָ֭מְרוּ מְתֵ֣י אׇהֳלִ֑י מִי־יִתֵּ֥ן מִ֝בְּשָׂר֗וֹ לֹ֣א נִשְׂבָּֽע׃
verses.douay_rheims: If the men of my tabernacle have not said: Who will give us of his flesh that we may be filled?
verses.finnish_biblia: Eivätkö miehet, jotka minun majassani ovat, sanoisi: jospa emme hänen lihastansa ravittaisi?
verses.king_james: If the men of my tabernacle said not, Oh that we had of his flesh! we cannot be satisfied.

verses.latin_vulgate: foris non mansit peregrinus :  ostium meum viatori patuit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּ֭חוּץ לֹא־יָלִ֣ין גֵּ֑ר דְּ֝לָתַ֗י לָאֹ֥רַח אֶפְתָּֽח׃
verses.douay_rheims: The stranger did not stay without, my door was open to the traveller.
verses.finnish_biblia: Muukalaisen ei pitänyt yötä ulkona oleman; vaan vaellusväelle avasin minä oveni.
verses.king_james: The stranger did not lodge in the street: but I opened my doors to the traveller.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si abscondi quasi homo peccatum meum,  et celavi in sinu meo iniquitatem meam ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־כִּסִּ֣יתִי כְאָדָ֣ם פְּשָׁעָ֑י לִטְמ֖וֹן בְּחֻבִּ֣י עֲוֺנִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: If as a man I have hid my sin, and have concealed my iniquity in my bosom.
verses.finnish_biblia: Olenko minä niinkuin ihminen peittänyt minun pahuuteni, salatakseni minun vääryyttäni?
verses.king_james: If I covered my transgressions as Adam, by hiding mine iniquity in my bosom:

verses.latin_vulgate: si expavi ad multitudinem nimiam,  et despectio propinquorum terruit me :  et non magis tacui, nec egressus sum ostium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י אֶעֱר֨וֹץ ׀ הָ֘מ֤וֹן רַבָּ֗ה וּבוּז־מִשְׁפָּח֥וֹת יְחִתֵּ֑נִי וָ֝אֶדֹּ֗ם לֹא־אֵ֥צֵא פָֽתַח׃
verses.douay_rheims: If I have been afraid at a very great multitude, and the contempt of kinsmen hath terrified me: and have not rather held my peace, and not gone out of the door.
verses.finnish_biblia: Olenko minä hämmästynyt suurta joukkoa? eli olenko minä sukulaisteni ylönkatsetta peljännyt? ollut ääneti, ja en mennyt ovesta ulos?
verses.king_james: Did I fear a great multitude, or did the contempt of families terrify me, that I kept silence, and went not out of the door?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quis mihi tribuat auditorem,  ut desiderium meum audiat Omnipotens,  et librum scribat ipse qui judicat,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִ֤י יִתֶּן־לִ֨י ׀ שֹׁ֘מֵ֤עַֽ לִ֗י הֶן־תָּ֭וִי שַׁדַּ֣י יַעֲנֵ֑נִי וְסֵ֥פֶר כָּ֝תַ֗ב אִ֣ישׁ רִיבִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Who would grant me a hearing, that the Almighty may hear my desire: and that he himself that judgeth would write a book,
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuka antais minulle kuultelian, että Kaikkivaltias kuulis minun pyyntöni, että joku kirjoittais kirjan minun asiastani;
verses.king_james: Oh that one would hear me! behold, my desire is, that the Almighty would answer me, and that mine adversary had written a book.

verses.latin_vulgate: ut in humero meo portem illum,  et circumdem illum quasi coronam mihi ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־לֹ֣א עַל־שִׁ֭כְמִי אֶשָּׂאֶ֑נּוּ אֶעֶנְדֶ֖נּוּ עֲטָר֣וֹת לִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: That I may carry it on my shoulder, and put it about me as a crown?
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin minä ottaisin sen hartioilleni, ja sitoisin ympärilleni niinkuin kruunun.
verses.king_james: Surely I would take it upon my shoulder, and bind it as a crown to me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Per singulos gradus meos pronuntiabo illum,  et quasi principi offeram eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִסְפַּ֣ר צְ֭עָדַי אַגִּידֶ֑נּוּ כְּמוֹ־נָ֝גִ֗יד אֲקָֽרְבֶֽנּוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: At every step of mine I would pronounce it, and offer it as to a prince.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä ilmoittaisin hänelle minun askeleini luvun, ja niinkuin ruhtinas kantaisin sen edes.
verses.king_james: I would declare unto him the number of my steps; as a prince would I go near unto him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si adversum me terra mea clamat,  et cum ipsa sulci ejus deflent :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־עָ֭לַי אַדְמָתִ֣י תִזְעָ֑ק וְ֝יַ֗חַד תְּלָמֶ֥יהָ יִבְכָּיֽוּן׃
verses.douay_rheims: If my land cry against me, and with it the furrows thereof mourn:
verses.finnish_biblia: Jos minun maani huutais minua vastaan, ja sen vaot kaikki ynnä itkisivät;
verses.king_james: If my land cry against me, or that the furrows likewise thereof complain;

verses.latin_vulgate: si fructus ejus comedi absque pecunia,  et animam agricolarum ejus afflixi :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־כֹּ֭חָהּ אָכַ֣לְתִּי בְלִי־כָ֑סֶף וְנֶ֖פֶשׁ בְּעָלֶ֣יהָ הִפָּֽחְתִּי׃
verses.douay_rheims: If I have eaten the fruits thereof without money, and have afflicted the son of the tillers thereof:
verses.finnish_biblia: Jos minä olen sen hedelmän maksamatta syönyt, ja tehnyt peltomiesten elämän työlääksi;
verses.king_james: If I have eaten the fruits thereof without money, or have caused the owners thereof to lose their life:

verses.latin_vulgate: pro frumento oriatur mihi tribulus,  et pro hordeo spina. Finita sunt verba Job.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תַּ֤חַת חִטָּ֨ה ׀ יֵ֥צֵא ח֗וֹחַ וְתַֽחַת־שְׂעֹרָ֥ה בׇאְשָׁ֑ה תַּ֝֗מּוּ דִּבְרֵ֥י אִיּֽוֹב׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: Let thistles grow up to me instead of wheat, and thorns instead of barley.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin kasvakoon minulle nisuista orjantappuria ja ohrista pahoja ruohoja. Jobin sanat loppuvat.
verses.king_james: Let thistles grow instead of wheat, and cockle instead of barley. The words of Job are ended.
